name: Yucatan Seafood Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 large Garlic head, roasted and chopped
2 quarts Fish stock OR 2 quarts Chicken stock
8 Large Tomatoes, grilled until almost black, roughly chopped
2 Pasilla chilies, roasted, peeled and with the seeds removed
8 Ounces Rock Cod, cut into 1-inch cubes
8 Ounces Ling Cod, cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a large sauté pan and add olive oil.
2. Sauté the onion until translucent, then add roasted garlic.
3. Add bay leaves and allspice, cooking about 10 minutes without browning.
4. In a saucepan, heat fish stock (or chicken stock).
5. To the pan, add coriander tied together, clams, and grilled tomatoes.
6. Increase heat to high and bring to a boil.
7. Add 2 cups of heated stock, cover, and steam until clams open (about 5 minutes). Top up with stock as needed.
8. Stir in lime juice and roasted garlic, then cook for 2 more minutes.
9. Bring mixture to a rolling boil and add mussels.
10. Add remaining fish, lobster, shrimp, oysters, and squid.
11. Remove the tied coriander before serving.
12. Cook covered for an additional 2-3 minutes.
13. Garnish with fresh coriander.
